title: EU Funding for the Development of Carbon Capture, Utilization and Storage (CCUS) in the Ã˜resund Region
content:
The EU has granted funding through Interreg Ã˜resundâ€“Kattegatâ€“Skagerrak to support a three-year project accelerating carbon capture, utilization and storage (CCUS) in the Ã˜resund region. The initiative seeks to integrate CCUS into the regional energy system by using captured biogenic COâ‚‚ together with green hydrogen and renewable energy to produce sustainable fuels for shipping and aviation. 
Key activities include analyzing CCUS value-chain synergies, developing markets, creating a green CCUS corridor, shaping political and policy strategies, promoting the Ã˜resund region internationally as a CCUS hub, and building financing and partnerships for future projects. Copenhagen MalmÃ¶ Port (CMP), via its innovation hub Ocean Valley, leads the consortium of energy, infrastructure, research, and business partners. The project strengthens CMPâ€™s role as a regional hub for CCUS logistics, supporting COâ‚‚ handling, storage, and integration with green energy. Overall, the initiative aims to establish lasting cross-border collaboration, accelerate CCUS deployment, and contribute to Europeâ€™s green transition and energy security.  With a total budget of over â‚¬1.2 million, including about â‚¬740,000 in EU funding, the project aims to move from analysis to implementation by establishing a cross-border Center of Excellence that combines Danish and Swedish strengths.
author: Analysts Account
